Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Personal Encyclopedias 632 upvotes, 129 comments
  2. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 43 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. European Parliament decided that Chat Control 1.0 must stop 506 upvotes, 118 comments
  4. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 74 upvotes, 12 comments
  5. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1372 upvotes, 367 comments
  6. Obsolete Sounds 148 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. Show HN: Claude skill that evaluates B2B vendors by talking to their AI agents 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Social-Media Addiction Trial 56 upvotes, 9 comments
  9. Cory Doctorow: Interoperability Can Save the Open Web 110 upvotes, 24 comments
  10. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. End of "Chat Control": EU Parliament Stops Mass Surveillance in Voting Thriller 290 upvotes, 82 comments
  12. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 775 upvotes, 262 comments
  13. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 207 upvotes, 61 comments
  14. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 199 upvotes, 60 comments
  15. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 45 upvotes, 11 comments
  16. OpenTelemetry Profiles Enters Public Alpha 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 362 upvotes, 166 comments
  18. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 278 upvotes, 133 comments
  19. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 58 upvotes, 22 comments
  20. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 52 upvotes, 21 comments
  21. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 198 upvotes, 106 comments
  22. ARC-AGI-3 468 upvotes, 301 comments
  23. Niche Museums 73 upvotes, 37 comments
  24. Swift 6.3 245 upvotes, 151 comments
  25. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 168 upvotes, 106 comments
  26. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  27. French e, è, é, ê, ë – what's the difference? 55 upvotes, 32 comments
  28. What came after the 486? 121 upvotes, 99 comments
  29. Newly purchased Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts to use smart features 130 upvotes, 125 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
